title: Eugenia yatuae
taxonomy: Eugenia yatuae is a species of flowering plant in the family Myrtaceae. It is native to the tropical regions of Central America. This plant belongs to the genus Eugenia, which comprises approximately 1,200 species of trees and shrubs. The family Myrtaceae is a large group of flowering plants that also includes clove and guava species. Eugenia yatuae is a member of the order Myrtales.

intro: Eugenia yattae, also known as the "Andina cherry" or "Ecuadorian cherry," is a species of flowering plant in the family Myrtaceae, native to the Andean region of South America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine due to its reported health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various conditions such as arthritis, digestive issues, and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Eugenia yattae include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and tannins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Eugenia yattae is commonly prepared as a tea or infusate, as well as in the form of capsules or extracts, and is used to support digestive health and reduce inflammation.
